1→# Agent Instructions
     2→
     3→This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
     4→
     5→## Quick Reference
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→bd ready              # Find available work
     9→bd show <id>          # View issue details
    10→bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
    11→bd close <id>         # Complete work
    12→bd sync               # Sync with git
    13→```
    14→
    15→## Issue Title Guidelines
    16→
    17→Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    18→is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    19→
    20→| Bad | Good |
    21→|---|---|
    22→| `Add support for filtering by SAT score` | `SAT score filter for search` |
    23→| `Update the search page to show result counts` | `Search result count display` |
    24→| `Fix bug where geographic radius is ignored` | `Geo radius filter ignored` |
    25→| `Implement new card component for institutions` | `Institution card component` |
    26→
    27→- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search …", "Auth …", "GeoField …"), not filler verbs
    28→- **Under 50 characters** — put detail in `--description`
    29→- Avoid prefixes like "Add support for", "Update the", "Implement new"
    30→
    31→## Creating Issues
    32→
    33→Use the correct issue type:
    34→- `epic` — a grouping container for related features/tasks. Not directly workable.
    35→- `feature` — delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    36→- `task` — scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work (e.g., project init, CI setup)
    37→- `bug` — something broken that needs fixing
    38→
    39→### Creating Epics
    40→
    41→Epics must use `--waits-for-gate` so they are blocked until their children complete, and do not appear in `bd ready`:
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→bd create "Epic: Feature Area" -t epic -p 2 -l label1,label2 \
    45→  -d "Description of the epic" \
    46→  --waits-for-gate all-children
    47→```
    48→
    49→This makes the epic resolve automatically when all children are closed. Never leave an epic as a bare open issue with no gate — it will pollute `bd ready`.
    50→
    51→### Creating Child Issues Under Epics
    52→
    53→Use `--parent` to place issues under an epic, and `--waits-for` to make the epic wait for the child:
    54→
    55→```bash
    56→bd create "Implement feature X" -t feature -p 1 -l search,go \
    57→  --parent kapow-abc \
    58→  --waits-for kapow-abc \
    59→  -d "Description" \
    60→  --acceptance "Acceptance criteria"
    61→```
    62→
    63→The `--parent` flag establishes the hierarchy. The `--waits-for kapow-abc` flag tells the epic to wait for this child before it can close.
    64→
    65→### Dependencies Between Issues
    66→
    67→Use `bd dep add` to express ordering constraints between issues:
    68→
    69→```bash
    70→bd dep add kapow-child kapow-dependency   # kapow-child is blocked by kapow-dependency
    71→```
    72→
    73→Only add dependencies where there is a real technical ordering constraint (e.g., "the migration must exist before the resolver can query the new column"). Do not add dependencies for soft preferences or nice-to-have ordering.
    74→
    75→## Implementation Plans
    76→
    77→Claude Code saves plans to `~/.claude/plans/` with random names (e.g.,
    78→`replicated-drifting-meerkat.md`). These are local to the machine and lost
    79→when switching devices. **After a plan is approved, copy it into the repo**
    80→so it persists across machines and sessions.
    81→
    82→### Saving a plan to the repo
    83→
    84→After exiting plan mode, copy the plan file from `~/.claude/plans/` into
    85→`docs/plans/`, renaming it to something meaningful:
    86→
    87→```bash
    88→cp ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    89→```
    90→
    91→Examples:
    92→- `docs/plans/kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`
    93→- `docs/plans/auth-refactor.md` (if no issue exists yet)
    94→
    95→Use lowercase kebab-case. Prefer `<issue-id>-<short-name>` when an issue exists.
    96→
    97→### Referencing from issues
    98→
    99→Add a note to the beads issue pointing to the plan:
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
   103→```
   104→
   105→### Resuming work from a plan
   106→
   107→When starting a new session, check `docs/plans/` for in-progress plans.
   108→If a beads issue references a plan file, read it before starting work.
   109→
   110→### Lifecycle
   111→
   112→- Commit and push plan files alongside the code they describe
   113→- Update plan files as the approach evolves during implementation
   114→- Delete plan files after the work is fully merged and closed
   115→
   116→## Completing an Issue
   117→
   118→An issue is not complete until:
   119→1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
   120→2. Changes are committed
   121→3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   122→4. Changes are pushed to remote with `bd sync && git push`
   123→
   124→## Git Workflow
   125→
   126→When work is complete and verified (tests pass, build succeeds, lint is clean),
   127→merge directly into `main`. Only fall back to a pull request if there is an
   128→unresolvable problem merging from the command line (e.g. complex conflicts).
   129→
   130→### Commit messages and issue references
   131→
   132→When a commit includes changes to beads data (`.beads/` files), list all
   133→affected issue IDs in the commit message body. This makes it easy to trace
   134→which issues were created, updated, or closed in each push.
   135→
   136→Example:
   137→```
   138→Add geo search field and update filter UI
   139→
   140→Implements proximity-based search using PostGIS ST_DWithin.
   141→
   142→Issues: kapow-a1b, kapow-c2d, kapow-e3f
   143→```
   144→
   145→### Worktree workflow
   146→
   147→When working in a git worktree (e.g. started with `--worktree`), **always merge
   148→the branch back into `main` before ending the session.** Do not leave the
   149→worktree branch unmerged without asking.
   150→
   151→**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   152→- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   153→- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging: `git rebase main` (from the worktree)
   154→
   155→**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   156→- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   157→- `.gitattributes` marks generated files with `merge=ours` — conflicts auto-resolve to the current branch, but you must regenerate to get the correct content
   158→
   159→**Worktree cleanup — never for Claude-managed worktrees:**
   160→Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically — both for `--worktree`
   161→sessions and for subagents using `isolation: "worktree"`. These worktrees are
   162→cleaned up on session/agent exit.
   163→
   164→**Rules:**
   165→- **Never** run `rm -rf` on any worktree directory — use `git worktree remove` instead, which properly updates git bookkeeping
   166→- **Never** run `git worktree remove` on a Claude-managed worktree (`.claude/worktrees/`) — let the session end naturally; manual removal mid-session breaks the working directory
   167→- `git worktree remove` is fine for non-Claude worktrees (e.g. user-created worktrees that need cleanup)
   168→
   169→## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
   170→
   171→**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
   172→
   173→**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
   174→
   175→1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
   176→2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
   177→3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
   178→4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   179→   ```bash
   180→   git pull --rebase
   181→   bd sync
   182→   git push
   183→   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   184→   ```
   185→5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
   186→6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
   187→7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
   188→
   189→**CRITICAL RULES:**
   190→- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
   191→- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
   192→- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
   193→- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
   194→
   195→
   196→<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   197→## Issue Tracking with bd (beads)
   198→
   199→**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **bd (beads)** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or other tracking methods.
   200→
   201→### Why bd?
   202→
   203→- Dependency-aware: Track blockers and relationships between issues
   204→- Git-friendly: Auto-syncs to JSONL for version control
   205→- Agent-optimized: JSON output, ready work detection, discovered-from links
   206→- Prevents duplicate tracking systems and confusion
   207→
   208→### Quick Start
   209→
   210→**Check for ready work:**
   211→
   212→```bash
   213→bd ready --json
   214→```
   215→
   216→**Create new issues:**
   217→
   218→```bash
   219→bd create "Issue title" --description="Detailed context" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
   220→bd create "Issue title" --description="What this issue is about" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-123 --json
   221→```
   222→
   223→**Claim and update:**
   224→
   225→```bash
   226→bd update bd-42 --status in_progress --json
   227→bd update bd-42 --priority 1 --json
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Complete work:**
   231→
   232→```bash
   233→bd close bd-42 --reason "Completed" --json
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Issue Types
   237→
   238→- `bug` - Something broken
   239→- `feature` - New functionality
   240→- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
   241→- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks
   242→- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)
   243→
   244→### Priorities
   245→
   246→- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
   247→- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
   248→- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)
   249→- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
   250→- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)
   251→
   252→### Workflow for AI Agents
   253→
   254→1. **Check ready work**: `bd ready` shows unblocked issues
   255→2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --status in_progress`
   256→3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
   257→4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue:
   258→   - `bd create "Found bug" --description="Details about what was found" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
   259→5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Done"`
   260→
   261→### Auto-Sync
   262→
   263→bd automatically syncs with git:
   264→
   265→- Exports to `.beads/issues.jsonl` after changes (5s debounce)
   266→- Imports from JSONL when newer (e.g., after `git pull`)
   267→- No manual export/import needed!
   268→
   269→### Important Rules
   270→
   271→- ✅ Use bd for ALL task tracking
   272→- ✅ Always use `--json` flag for programmatic use
   273→- ✅ Link discovered work with `discovered-from` dependencies
   274→- ✅ Check `bd ready` before asking "what should I work on?"
   275→- ❌ Do NOT create markdown TODO lists
   276→- ❌ Do NOT use external issue trackers
   277→- ❌ Do NOT duplicate tracking systems
   278→
   279→For more details, see README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md.
   280→
   281→<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   282→

1→import { type Page, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Shared E2E test utilities for Kapow.
     5→ *
     6→ * Tagging convention for test titles:
     7→ *
     8→ *   Tiers (pick one):
     9→ *     @critical        — must-pass smoke tests; blocks deployment
    10→ *     @standard        — normal regression coverage
    11→ *     @comprehensive   — edge cases, visual, slow scenarios
    12→ *
    13→ *   Features (pick one or more):
    14→ *     @search    @filters    @pagination   @sorting
    15→ *     @views     @detail     @columns      @geo
    16→ *     @blend     @export     @auth         @nav
    17→ *     @responsive
    18→ *
    19→ *   Example test title:
    20→ *     'loads search page and displays results @critical @search'
    21→ */
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Navigate to the /search page with the given URL search params.
    25→ *
    26→ * @example
    27→ *   await searchWith(page, { q: 'MIT', state: 'MA' })
    28→ *   // navigates to /search?q=MIT&state=MA
    29→ */
    30→export async function searchWith(
    31→  page: Page,
    32→  params: Record<string, string>,
    33→): Promise<void> {
    34→  const search = new URLSearchParams(params).toString()
    35→  const url = search ? `/search?${search}` : '/search'
    36→  await page.goto(url)
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Wait for the search results skeleton to clear and results to become visible.
    41→ * Asserts that the loading indicator disappears and at least one result or
    42→ * the "institutions found" / "results" text is visible.
    43→ */
    44→export async function waitForResults(page: Page): Promise<void> {
    45→  // Wait for the loading skeleton to disappear
    46→  await expect(page.getByTestId('results-loading')).toBeHidden({
    47→    timeout: 30_000,
    48→  })
    49→
    50→  // Wait for the results summary text to appear
    51→  // Matches both "Showing 1-20 of 42 institutions found" and "100+ results"
    52→  await expect(
    53→    page.getByText(/institutions found|results/i),
    54→  ).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 })
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Parse the displayed result count from the results summary text.
    59→ * Handles both "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" and "100+ results" formats.
    60→ *
    61→ * @returns The total number of results, or 101 for the "100+" display.
    62→ */
    63→export async function getResultCount(page: Page): Promise<number> {
    64→  const summaryLocator = page.getByText(/Showing \d+/i)
    65→  const text = await summaryLocator.textContent()
    66→
    67→  if (!text) {
    68→    throw new Error('Could not find results summary text on page')
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  // Match "100+ results" format
    72→  const plusMatch = text.match(/(\d+)\+\s*results/i)
    73→  if (plusMatch) {
    74→    return Number(plusMatch[1]) + 1
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  // Match "Showing X-Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X-Y of Z,ZZZ institutions found"
    78→  const ofMatch = text.match(/of\s+([\d,]+)/i)
    79→  if (ofMatch) {
    80→    return Number(ofMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ''))
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  throw new Error(`Could not parse result count from text: "${text}"`)
    84→}

     3→.PHONY: install dev build lint format clean test test-coverage e2e-install e2e-test e2e-test-critical screenshots storybook
     4→
     5→## install: Install dependencies
     6→install:
     7→	pnpm install
     8→
     9→## dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080)
    10→dev:
    11→	pnpm dev
    12→
    13→## build: Type-check and build for production
    14→build:
    15→	pnpm build
    16→
    17→## lint: Run ESLint
    18→lint:
    19→	pnpm lint
    20→
    21→## format: Run Prettier
    22→format:
    23→	pnpm format
    24→
    25→## preview: Preview production build locally
    26→preview:
    27→	pnpm preview
    28→
    29→## test: Run unit tests
    30→test:
    31→	pnpm test
    32→
    33→## test-coverage: Run unit tests with coverage report
    34→test-coverage:
    35→	pnpm test:coverage
    36→
    37→## e2e-install: Install Playwright browser binaries (run once after pnpm install)
    38→e2e-install:
    39→	pnpm exec playwright install chromium
    40→
    41→## e2e-test: Run Playwright end-to-end tests (starts Vite dev server automatically)
    42→e2e-test:
    43→	pnpm test:e2e
    44→
    45→## e2e-test-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
    46→e2e-test-critical:
    47→	pnpm test:e2e --grep @critical
    48→
    49→## screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
    50→screenshots:
    51→	mkdir -p e2e/screenshots
    52→	pnpm exec playwright test e2e/screenshots.spec.ts
    53→
    54→## storybook: Start Storybook dev server (port 6006)
    55→storybook:
    56→	pnpm storybook
    57→
    58→## clean: Remove build artifacts and node_modules
    59→clean:
    60→	rm -rf dist node_modules .pnpm-store
    61→

1→import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→import { searchWith, waitForResults, getResultCount } from './helpers'
     3→
     4→test.describe('Pagination', () => {
     5→  test('result count displayed (Showing X-Y of Z institutions) @critical @pagination', async ({
     6→    page,
     7→  }) => {
     8→    await searchWith(page, {})
     9→    await waitForResults(page)
    10→
    11→    // The summary text should match "Showing X–Y of Z institutions found" or "Showing X–Y of Z+ results"
    12→    const summary = page.getByText(/Showing \d+[–-]\d+ of/i)
    13→    await expect(summary).toBeVisible()
    14→
    15→    // Verify the count is parseable
    16→    const count = await getResultCount(page)
    17→    expect(count).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    18→  })
    19→
    20→  test('click page 2 shows different results and URL updates with page=2 @critical @pagination', async ({
    21→    page,
    22→  }) => {
    23→    await searchWith(page, {})
    24→    await waitForResults(page)
    25→
    26→    // Collect first page institution names
    27→    const summaryBefore = await page.getByText(/Showing \d+[–-]\d+ of/i).textContent()
    28→
    29→    // Click page 2 in pagination controls
    30→    const paginationControls = page.getByTestId('pagination-controls')
    31→    await expect(paginationControls).toBeVisible()
    32→    await paginationControls.getByRole('button', { name: '2' }).click()
    33→
    34→    // Wait for results to update
    35→    await waitForResults(page)
    36→
    37→    // URL should contain page=2
    38→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/[?&]page=2/)
    39→
    40→    // Summary text should show a different range (e.g., "Showing 21–40" instead of "Showing 1–20")
    41→    const summaryAfter = await page.getByText(/Showing \d+[–-]\d+ of/i).textContent()
    42→    expect(summaryAfter).not.toBe(summaryBefore)
    43→  })
    44→
    45→  test('change page size updates results per page and resets to page 1 @standard @pagination', async ({
    46→    page,
    47→  }) => {
    48→    // Start on page 2 so we can verify reset
    49→    await searchWith(page, { page: '2' })
    50→    await waitForResults(page)
    51→
    52→    // Change page size via the "Per page" select
    53→    const pageSizeSelect = page.getByLabel('Per page')
    54→    await pageSizeSelect.selectOption('5')
    55→
    56→    // Wait for results to update
    57→    await waitForResults(page)
    58→
    59→    // URL should have pageSize=5 and page should be reset (no page param or page=1)
    60→    const url = page.url()
    61→    expect(url).toContain('pageSize=5')
    62→    expect(url).not.toMatch(/[?&]page=/)
    63→
    64→    // Summary should show at most 5 results in the range
    65→    const summary = await page.getByText(/Showing \d+[–-]\d+ of/i).textContent()
    66→    expect(summary).not.toBeNull()
    67→    const rangeMatch = summary!.match(/Showing (\d+)[–-](\d+)/)
    68→    expect(rangeMatch).not.toBeNull()
    69→    const rangeSize = Number(rangeMatch![2]) - Number(rangeMatch![1]) + 1
    70→    expect(rangeSize).toBeLessThanOrEqual(5)
    71→  })
    72→
    73→  test('pagination controls hidden when all results fit on one page @comprehensive @pagination', async ({
    74→    page,
    75→  }) => {
    76→    // Use a very specific search that returns few results, with a large page size
    77→    await searchWith(page, { q: 'Massachusetts Institute of Technology', pageSize: '100' })
    78→    await waitForResults(page)
    79→
    80→    const count = await getResultCount(page)
    81→    if (count <= 100) {
    82→      // Pagination controls should not be visible
    83→      await expect(page.getByTestId('pagination-controls')).toBeHidden()
    84→    } else {
    85→      // If somehow there are more than 100 results, pagination should be visible
    86→      await expect(page.getByTestId('pagination-controls')).toBeVisible()
    87→    }
    88→  })
    89→})
    90→
    91→test.describe('Sorting', () => {
    92→  test('click sortable column header in table view reorders results and updates URL @standard @sorting', async ({
    93→    page,
    94→  }) => {
    95→    // Navigate to search in table view
    96→    await searchWith(page, { view: 'table' })
    97→    await waitForResults(page)
    98→
    99→    // Click the "Name" sort header
   100→    const sortButton = page.getByTestId('sort-name')
   101→    await expect(sortButton).toBeVisible()
   102→    await sortButton.click()
   103→
   104→    // Wait for results to update
   105→    await waitForResults(page)
   106→
   107→    // URL should contain sort=name
   108→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/[?&]sort=name/)
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  test('click same header again toggles sort direction ASC to DESC @standard @sorting', async ({
   112→    page,
   113→  }) => {
   114→    // Start with sort=name (ASC by default)
   115→    await searchWith(page, { view: 'table', sort: 'name' })
   116→    await waitForResults(page)
   117→
   118→    // Click name header again to toggle to DESC
   119→    const sortButton = page.getByTestId('sort-name')
   120→    await sortButton.click()
   121→    await waitForResults(page)
   122→
   123→    // URL should now have sortDir=DESC
   124→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/[?&]sortDir=DESC/)
   125→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/[?&]sort=name/)
   126→  })
   127→
   128→  test('URL reflects both page number and sort state @comprehensive @sorting @pagination', async ({
   129→    page,
   130→  }) => {
   131→    // Navigate with sort active
   132→    await searchWith(page, { view: 'table', sort: 'name' })
   133→    await waitForResults(page)
   134→
   135→    // Click page 2
   136→    const paginationControls = page.getByTestId('pagination-controls')
   137→    await expect(paginationControls).toBeVisible()
   138→    await paginationControls.getByRole('button', { name: '2' }).click()
   139→    await waitForResults(page)
   140→
   141→    // URL should contain both sort=name and page=2
   142→    const url = page.url()
   143→    expect(url).toMatch(/[?&]sort=name/)
   144→    expect(url).toMatch(/[?&]page=2/)
   145→  })
   146→
   147→  test('sort column headers disabled when ranking blend is active @comprehensive @sorting @blend', async ({
   148→    page,
   149→  }) => {
   150→    // Navigate to table view with a blend weight active
   151→    // The blend param format is "field:weight" (e.g., "admissionRate:50")
   152→    await searchWith(page, { view: 'table', blend: 'admissionRate:50' })
   153→    await waitForResults(page)
   154→
   155→    // When blend is active, sort is passed as null — the onSort prop is not provided
   156→    // and header buttons should render without sort interactivity.
   157→    // The sort URL params should not be present (blend clears them).
   158→    const url = page.url()
   159→    expect(url).not.toMatch(/[?&]sort=/)
   160→    expect(url).not.toMatch(/[?&]sortDir=/)
   161→  })
   162→})

     6→test.describe('Navigation & Deep Links', () => {
     7→  test('navigate directly to /search?state=CA pre-populates filter and shows results @critical @nav', async ({
     8→    page,
     9→  }) => {
    10→    await searchWith(page, { state: 'CA' })
    11→    await waitForResults(page)
    12→
    13→    // State filter should be pre-populated
    14→    const stateSelect = page
    15→      .locator('label', { has: page.getByText('State') })
    16→      .locator('select')
    17→    await expect(stateSelect).toHaveValue('CA')
    18→
    19→    // Results should be showing
    20→    const count = await getResultCount(page)
    21→    expect(count).toBeGreaterThan(0)
    22→  })
    23→
    24→  // ── Standard ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→
    26→  test('header nav: Home to About to Home via nav links @standard @nav', async ({
    27→    page,
    28→  }) => {
    29→    await page.goto('/')
    30→
    31→    // Click About link
    32→    await page.getByRole('link', { name: 'About' }).click()
    33→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/about')
    34→
    35→    // Click Home link
    36→    await page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Home' }).click()
    37→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/')
    38→  })
    39→
    40→  test('logo click returns to home from any page @standard @nav', async ({
    41→    page,
    42→  }) => {
    43→    // Navigate to the about page
    44→    await page.goto('/about')
    45→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/about')
    46→
    47→    // Click the logo/wordmark link
    48→    await page
    49→      .getByRole('link', { name: /College Power Search/i })
    50→      .first()
    51→      .click()
    52→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/')
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  // ── Comprehensive ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→
    57→  test('all filters round-trip through URL @comprehensive @nav', async ({
    58→    page,
    59→  }) => {
    60→    // Set multiple filters
    61→    await searchWith(page, { state: 'NY', enrollment: '1000,10000' })
    62→    await waitForResults(page)
    63→    const count1 = await getResultCount(page)
    64→
    65→    // Copy the current URL
    66→    const currentUrl = page.url()
    67→
    68→    // Navigate away
    69→    await page.goto('/')
    70→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/')
    71→
    72→    // Navigate back to the saved URL
    73→    await page.goto(currentUrl)
    74→    await waitForResults(page)
    75→
    76→    // Filters should be restored — same result count
    77→    const count2 = await getResultCount(page)
    78→    expect(count2).toBe(count1)
    79→
    80→    // URL should contain the same params

    82→    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/enrollment=1000,10000/)

   115→    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })
   116→    await searchWith(page, {})

   119→    // Look for a filter toggle button

   123→
   124→    // If the filter toggle exists on mobile, test it

   128→      // Click to toggle filter visibility
   129→      await filterToggle.click()
   130→
   131→      // Verify the State filter label is visible (filters are shown)

1→# Agent Instructions
     2→
     3→This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
     4→
     5→## Quick Reference
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→bd ready              # Find available work
     9→bd show <id>          # View issue details
    10→bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
    11→bd close <id>         # Complete work
    12→bd sync               # Sync with git
    13→```
    14→
    15→## Issue Title Guidelines
    16→
    17→Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    18→is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    19→
    20→| Bad | Good |
    21→|---|---|
    22→| `Add support for filtering by SAT score` | `SAT score filter for search` |
    23→| `Update the search page to show result counts` | `Search result count display` |
    24→| `Fix bug where geographic radius is ignored` | `Geo radius filter ignored` |
    25→| `Implement new card component for institutions` | `Institution card component` |
    26→
    27→- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search …", "Auth …", "GeoField …"), not filler verbs
    28→- **Under 50 characters** — put detail in `--description`
    29→- Avoid prefixes like "Add support for", "Update the", "Implement new"
    30→
    31→## Creating Issues
    32→
    33→Use the correct issue type:
    34→- `epic` — a grouping container for related features/tasks. Not directly workable.
    35→- `feature` — delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    36→- `task` — scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work (e.g., project init, CI setup)
    37→- `bug` — something broken that needs fixing
    38→
    39→### Creating Epics
    40→
    41→Epics must use `--waits-for-gate` so they are blocked until their children complete, and do not appear in `bd ready`:
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→bd create "Epic: Feature Area" -t epic -p 2 -l label1,label2 \
    45→  -d "Description of the epic" \
    46→  --waits-for-gate all-children
    47→```
    48→
    49→This makes the epic resolve automatically when all children are closed. Never leave an epic as a bare open issue with no gate — it will pollute `bd ready`.
    50→
    51→### Creating Child Issues Under Epics
    52→
    53→Use `--parent` to place issues under an epic, and `--waits-for` to make the epic wait for the child:
    54→
    55→```bash
    56→bd create "Implement feature X" -t feature -p 1 -l search,go \
    57→  --parent kapow-abc \
    58→  --waits-for kapow-abc \
    59→  -d "Description" \
    60→  --acceptance "Acceptance criteria"
    61→```
    62→
    63→The `--parent` flag establishes the hierarchy. The `--waits-for kapow-abc` flag tells the epic to wait for this child before it can close.
    64→
    65→### Dependencies Between Issues
    66→
    67→Use `bd dep add` to express ordering constraints between issues:
    68→
    69→```bash
    70→bd dep add kapow-child kapow-dependency   # kapow-child is blocked by kapow-dependency
    71→```
    72→
    73→Only add dependencies where there is a real technical ordering constraint (e.g., "the migration must exist before the resolver can query the new column"). Do not add dependencies for soft preferences or nice-to-have ordering.
    74→
    75→## Implementation Plans
    76→
    77→Claude Code saves plans to `~/.claude/plans/` with random names (e.g.,
    78→`replicated-drifting-meerkat.md`). These are local to the machine and lost
    79→when switching devices. **After a plan is approved, copy it into the repo**
    80→so it persists across machines and sessions.
    81→
    82→### Saving a plan to the repo
    83→
    84→After exiting plan mode, copy the plan file from `~/.claude/plans/` into
    85→`docs/plans/`, renaming it to something meaningful:
    86→
    87→```bash
    88→cp ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    89→```
    90→
    91→Examples:
    92→- `docs/plans/kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`
    93→- `docs/plans/auth-refactor.md` (if no issue exists yet)
    94→
    95→Use lowercase kebab-case. Prefer `<issue-id>-<short-name>` when an issue exists.
    96→
    97→### Referencing from issues
    98→
    99→Add a note to the beads issue pointing to the plan:
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
   103→```
   104→
   105→### Resuming work from a plan
   106→
   107→When starting a new session, check `docs/plans/` for in-progress plans.
   108→If a beads issue references a plan file, read it before starting work.
   109→
   110→### Lifecycle
   111→
   112→- Commit and push plan files alongside the code they describe
   113→- Update plan files as the approach evolves during implementation
   114→- Delete plan files after the work is fully merged and closed
   115→
   116→## Completing an Issue
   117→
   118→An issue is not complete until:
   119→1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
   120→2. Changes are committed
   121→3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   122→4. Changes are pushed to remote with `bd sync && git push`
   123→
   124→## Git Workflow
   125→
   126→When work is complete and verified (tests pass, build succeeds, lint is clean),
   127→merge directly into `main`. Only fall back to a pull request if there is an
   128→unresolvable problem merging from the command line (e.g. complex conflicts).
   129→
   130→### Commit messages and issue references
   131→
   132→When a commit includes changes to beads data (`.beads/` files), list all
   133→affected issue IDs in the commit message body. This makes it easy to trace
   134→which issues were created, updated, or closed in each push.
   135→
   136→Example:
   137→```
   138→Add geo search field and update filter UI
   139→
   140→Implements proximity-based search using PostGIS ST_DWithin.
   141→
   142→Issues: kapow-a1b, kapow-c2d, kapow-e3f
   143→```
   144→
   145→### Worktree workflow
   146→
   147→When working in a git worktree (e.g. started with `--worktree`), **always merge
   148→the branch back into `main` before ending the session.** Do not leave the
   149→worktree branch unmerged without asking.
   150→
   151→**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   152→- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   153→- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging: `git rebase main` (from the worktree)
   154→
   155→**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   156→- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   157→- `.gitattributes` marks generated files with `merge=ours` — conflicts auto-resolve to the current branch, but you must regenerate to get the correct content
   158→
   159→**Worktree cleanup — never for Claude-managed worktrees:**
   160→Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically — both for `--worktree`
   161→sessions and for subagents using `isolation: "worktree"`. These worktrees are
   162→cleaned up on session/agent exit.
   163→
   164→**Rules:**
   165→- **Never** run `rm -rf` on any worktree directory — use `git worktree remove` instead, which properly updates git bookkeeping
   166→- **Never** run `git worktree remove` on a Claude-managed worktree (`.claude/worktrees/`) — let the session end naturally; manual removal mid-session breaks the working directory
   167→- `git worktree remove` is fine for non-Claude worktrees (e.g. user-created worktrees that need cleanup)
   168→
   169→## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
   170→
   171→**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
   172→
   173→**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
   174→
   175→1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
   176→2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
   177→3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
   178→4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   179→   ```bash
   180→   git pull --rebase
   181→   bd sync
   182→   git push
   183→   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   184→   ```
   185→5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
   186→6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
   187→7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
   188→
   189→**CRITICAL RULES:**
   190→- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
   191→- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
   192→- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
   193→- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
   194→
   195→
   196→<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   197→## Issue Tracking with bd (beads)
   198→
   199→**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **bd (beads)** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or other tracking methods.
   200→
   201→### Why bd?
   202→
   203→- Dependency-aware: Track blockers and relationships between issues
   204→- Git-friendly: Auto-syncs to JSONL for version control
   205→- Agent-optimized: JSON output, ready work detection, discovered-from links
   206→- Prevents duplicate tracking systems and confusion
   207→
   208→### Quick Start
   209→
   210→**Check for ready work:**
   211→
   212→```bash
   213→bd ready --json
   214→```
   215→
   216→**Create new issues:**
   217→
   218→```bash
   219→bd create "Issue title" --description="Detailed context" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
   220→bd create "Issue title" --description="What this issue is about" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-123 --json
   221→```
   222→
   223→**Claim and update:**
   224→
   225→```bash
   226→bd update bd-42 --status in_progress --json
   227→bd update bd-42 --priority 1 --json
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Complete work:**
   231→
   232→```bash
   233→bd close bd-42 --reason "Completed" --json
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Issue Types
   237→
   238→- `bug` - Something broken
   239→- `feature` - New functionality
   240→- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
   241→- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks
   242→- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)
   243→
   244→### Priorities
   245→
   246→- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
   247→- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
   248→- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)
   249→- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
   250→- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)
   251→
   252→### Workflow for AI Agents
   253→
   254→1. **Check ready work**: `bd ready` shows unblocked issues
   255→2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --status in_progress`
   256→3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
   257→4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue:
   258→   - `bd create "Found bug" --description="Details about what was found" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
   259→5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Done"`
   260→
   261→### Auto-Sync
   262→
   263→bd automatically syncs with git:
   264→
   265→- Exports to `.beads/issues.jsonl` after changes (5s debounce)
   266→- Imports from JSONL when newer (e.g., after `git pull`)
   267→- No manual export/import needed!
   268→
   269→### Important Rules
   270→
   271→- ✅ Use bd for ALL task tracking
   272→- ✅ Always use `--json` flag for programmatic use
   273→- ✅ Link discovered work with `discovered-from` dependencies
   274→- ✅ Check `bd ready` before asking "what should I work on?"
   275→- ❌ Do NOT create markdown TODO lists
   276→- ❌ Do NOT use external issue trackers
   277→- ❌ Do NOT duplicate tracking systems
   278→
   279→For more details, see README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md.
   280→
   281→<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   282→

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  test-standard:
    name: Standard quality gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: "1.23"

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: web/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v6
        with:
          version: latest
          install-mode: binary
          args: --timeout=5m
          working-directory: api
          skip-cache: false

      - name: Run standard quality gate
        run: make test-standard
